name: subfinder
title: subfinder

commands.name: subfinder

arguments.description: domains to find subdomains for
arguments.raw: -d, -domain string[]  domains to find subdomains for

arguments.description: file containing list of domains for subdomain discovery
arguments.raw: -dL, -list string     file containing list of domains for subdomain discovery

arguments.description: specific sources to use for discovery (-s crtsh,github). Use -ls to display all available sources.
arguments.raw: -s, -sources string[]           specific sources to use for discovery (-s crtsh,github). Use -ls to display all available sources.

arguments.description: use only sources that can handle subdomains recursively rather than both recursive and non-recursive sources
arguments.raw: -recursive                      use only sources that can handle subdomains recursively rather than both recursive and non-recursive sources

arguments.description: use all sources for enumeration (slow)
arguments.raw: -all                            use all sources for enumeration (slow)

arguments.description: sources to exclude from enumeration (-es alienvault,zoomeyeapi)
arguments.raw: -es, -exclude-sources string[]  sources to exclude from enumeration (-es alienvault,zoomeyeapi)

arguments.description: subdomain or list of subdomain to match (file or comma separated)
arguments.raw: -m, -match string[]   subdomain or list of subdomain to match (file or comma separated)

arguments.description: subdomain or list of subdomain to filter (file or comma separated)
arguments.raw: -f, -filter string[]   subdomain or list of subdomain to filter (file or comma separated)

arguments.description: maximum number of http requests to send per second (global)
arguments.raw: -rl, -rate-limit int      maximum number of http requests to send per second (global)

arguments.description: maximum number of http requests to send per second for providers in key=value format (-rls hackertarget=10/m) (default ["github=30/m", "fullhunt=60/m", "pugrecon=10/s", "robtex=18446744073709551615/ms", "securitytrails=1/s", "shodan=1/s", "virustotal=4/m", "hackertarget=2/s", "waybackarchive=15/m", "whoisxmlapi=50/s", "securitytrails=2/s", "sitedossier=8/m", "netlas=1/s", "github=83/m", "hudsonrock=5/s", "urlscan=1/s"])
arguments.raw: -rls, -rate-limits value  maximum number of http requests to send per second for providers in key=value format (-rls hackertarget=10/m) (default ["github=30/m", "fullhunt=60/m", "pugrecon=10/s", "robtex=18446744073709551615/ms", "securitytrails=1/s", "shodan=1/s", "virustotal=4/m", "hackertarget=2/s", "waybackarchive=15/m", "whoisxmlapi=50/s", "securitytrails=2/s", "sitedossier=8/m", "netlas=1/s", "github=83/m", "hudsonrock=5/s", "urlscan=1/s"])

arguments.description: number of concurrent goroutines for resolving (-active only) (default 10)
arguments.raw: -t int                    number of concurrent goroutines for resolving (-active only) (default 10)

arguments.description: update subfinder to latest version
arguments.raw: -up, -update                 update subfinder to latest version

arguments.description: disable automatic subfinder update check
arguments.raw: -duc, -disable-update-check  disable automatic subfinder update check

arguments.description: file to write output to
arguments.raw: -o, -output string       file to write output to

arguments.description: write output in JSONL(ines) format
arguments.raw: -oJ, -json               write output in JSONL(ines) format

arguments.description: directory to write output (-dL only)
arguments.raw: -oD, -output-dir string  directory to write output (-dL only)

arguments.description: include all sources in the output (-json only)
arguments.raw: -cs, -collect-sources    include all sources in the output (-json only)

arguments.description: include host IP in output (-active only)
arguments.raw: -oI, -ip                 include host IP in output (-active only)

arguments.description: flag config file (default "/root/.config/subfinder/config.yaml")
arguments.raw: -config string                flag config file (default "/root/.config/subfinder/config.yaml")

arguments.description: provider config file (default "/root/.config/subfinder/provider-config.yaml")
arguments.raw: -pc, -provider-config string  provider config file (default "/root/.config/subfinder/provider-config.yaml")

arguments.description: comma separated list of resolvers to use
arguments.raw: -r string[]                   comma separated list of resolvers to use

arguments.description: file containing list of resolvers to use
arguments.raw: -rL, -rlist string            file containing list of resolvers to use

arguments.description: display active subdomains only
arguments.raw: -nW, -active                  display active subdomains only

arguments.description: http proxy to use with subfinder
arguments.raw: -proxy string                 http proxy to use with subfinder

arguments.description: exclude IPs from the list of domains
arguments.raw: -ei, -exclude-ip              exclude IPs from the list of domains

arguments.description: show only subdomains in output
arguments.raw: -silent             show only subdomains in output

arguments.description: show version of subfinder
arguments.raw: -version            show version of subfinder

arguments.description: show verbose output
arguments.raw: -v                  show verbose output

arguments.description: disable color in output
arguments.raw: -nc, -no-color      disable color in output

arguments.description: list all available sources
arguments.raw: -ls, -list-sources  list all available sources

arguments.description: report source statistics
arguments.raw: -stats              report source statistics

arguments.description: seconds to wait before timing out (default 30)
arguments.raw: -timeout int   seconds to wait before timing out (default 30)

arguments.description: minutes to wait for enumeration results (default 10)
arguments.raw: -max-time int  minutes to wait for enumeration results (default 10)

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# subfinder -h
 Subfinder is a subdomain discovery tool that discovers subdomains for websites by using passive online sources.
 
 Usage:
 subfinder [flags]
 
 Flags:
 INPUT:
  -d, -domain string[]  domains to find subdomains for
  -dL, -list string     file containing list of domains for subdomain discovery
 
 SOURCE:
  -s, -sources string[]           specific sources to use for discovery (-s crtsh,github). Use -ls to display all available sources.
  -recursive                      use only sources that can handle subdomains recursively rather than both recursive and non-recursive sources
  -all                            use all sources for enumeration (slow)
  -es, -exclude-sources string[]  sources to exclude from enumeration (-es alienvault,zoomeyeapi)
 
 FILTER:
  -m, -match string[]   subdomain or list of subdomain to match (file or comma separated)
  -f, -filter string[]   subdomain or list of subdomain to filter (file or comma separated)
 
 RATE-LIMIT:
  -rl, -rate-limit int      maximum number of http requests to send per second (global)
  -rls, -rate-limits value  maximum number of http requests to send per second for providers in key=value format (-rls hackertarget=10/m) (default ["github=30/m", "fullhunt=60/m", "pugrecon=10/s", "robtex=18446744073709551615/ms", "securitytrails=1/s", "shodan=1/s", "virustotal=4/m", "hackertarget=2/s", "waybackarchive=15/m", "whoisxmlapi=50/s", "securitytrails=2/s", "sitedossier=8/m", "netlas=1/s", "github=83/m", "hudsonrock=5/s", "urlscan=1/s"])
  -t int                    number of concurrent goroutines for resolving (-active only) (default 10)
 
 UPDATE:
  -up, -update                 update subfinder to latest version
  -duc, -disable-update-check  disable automatic subfinder update check
 
 OUTPUT:
  -o, -output string       file to write output to
  -oJ, -json               write output in JSONL(ines) format
  -oD, -output-dir string  directory to write output (-dL only)
  -cs, -collect-sources    include all sources in the output (-json only)
  -oI, -ip                 include host IP in output (-active only)
 
 CONFIGURATION:
  -config string                flag config file (default "/root/.config/subfinder/config.yaml")
  -pc, -provider-config string  provider config file (default "/root/.config/subfinder/provider-config.yaml")
  -r string[]                   comma separated list of resolvers to use
  -rL, -rlist string            file containing list of resolvers to use
  -nW, -active                  display active subdomains only
  -proxy string                 http proxy to use with subfinder
  -ei, -exclude-ip              exclude IPs from the list of domains
 
 DEBUG:
  -silent             show only subdomains in output
  -version            show version of subfinder
  -v                  show verbose output
  -nc, -no-color      disable color in output
  -ls, -list-sources  list all available sources
  -stats              report source statistics
 
 OPTIMIZATION:
  -timeout int   seconds to wait before timing out (default 30)
  -max-time int  minutes to wait for enumeration results (default 10)

local_help.--help:
Subfinder is a subdomain discovery tool that discovers subdomains for websites by using passive online sources.

Usage:
  subfinder [flags]

Flags:
INPUT:
   -d, -domain string[]  domains to find subdomains for
   -dL, -list string     file containing list of domains for subdomain discovery

SOURCE:
   -s, -sources string[]           specific sources to use for discovery (-s crtsh,github). Use -ls to display all available sources.
   -recursive                      use only sources that can handle subdomains recursively rather than both recursive and non-recursive sources
   -all                            use all sources for enumeration (slow)
   -es, -exclude-sources string[]  sources to exclude from enumeration (-es alienvault,zoomeyeapi)

FILTER:
   -m, -match string[]   subdomain or list of subdomain to match (file or comma separated)
   -f, -filter string[]   subdomain or list of subdomain to filter (file or comma separated)

RATE-LIMIT:
   -rl, -rate-limit int      maximum number of http requests to send per second (global)
   -rls, -rate-limits value  maximum number of http requests to send per second for providers in key=value format (-rls hackertarget=10/m) (default ["github=30/m", "fullhunt=60/m", "pugrecon=10/s", "robtex=18446744073709551615/ms", "securitytrails=1/s", "shodan=1/s", "virustotal=4/m", "hackertarget=2/s", "waybackarchive=15/m", "whoisxmlapi=50/s", "securitytrails=2/s", "sitedossier=8/m", "netlas=1/s", "github=83/m", "hudsonrock=5/s", "urlscan=1/s"])
   -t int                    number of concurrent goroutines for resolving (-active only) (default 10)

UPDATE:
   -up, -update                 update subfinder to latest version
   -duc, -disable-update-check  disable automatic subfinder update check

OUTPUT:
   -o, -output string       file to write output to
   -oJ, -json               write output in JSONL(ines) format
   -oD, -output-dir string  directory to write output (-dL only)
   -cs, -collect-sources    include all sources in the output (-json only)
   -oI, -ip                 include host IP in output (-active only)

CONFIGURATION:
   -config string                flag config file (default "/root/.config/subfinder/config.yaml")
   -pc, -provider-config string  provider config file (default "/root/.config/subfinder/provider-config.yaml")
   -r string[]                   comma separated list of resolvers to use
   -rL, -rlist string            file containing list of resolvers to use
   -nW, -active                  display active subdomains only
   -proxy string                 http proxy to use with subfinder
   -ei, -exclude-ip              exclude IPs from the list of domains

DEBUG:
   -silent             show only subdomains in output
   -version            show version of subfinder
   -v                  show verbose output
   -nc, -no-color      disable color in output
   -ls, -list-sources  list all available sources
   -stats              report source statistics

OPTIMIZATION:
   -timeout int   seconds to wait before timing out (default 30)
   -max-time int  minutes to wait for enumeration results (default 10)

apt_metadata.description:
subdomain discovery tool
This package contains a subdomain discovery tool that discovers valid
subdomains for websites by using passive online sources. It has a simple
modular architecture and is optimized for speed. subfinder is built for doing
one thing only - passive subdomain enumeration, and it does that very well.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
